"'''Double Cross on Ord Mantell'''" is a short story by [[Michael Mikaelian]] that originally appeared in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 5|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 5]]. It was added to [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] on [[October 15]], [[2004]].

==Plot summary==
A [[Nalrithian]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] named [[Phoedris Bos]] is stalked and [[Death/Legends|killed]] by his egg mate, [[Cypher Bos]], who then poses as him. Phoedris was arranging the capture of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] shipment of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to help fund the Rebel outpost on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].

==Continuity==
This short story expanded upon ''[[Rebel Mission to Ord Mantell]]'', an audio dramatization written by [[Brian Daley]] and released by [[Buena Vista Records]] in [[1983]]. The audio drama was one of the several stories attempting to explain who “the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] on [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]]” was that [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] tangled with between the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Episode IV]] and [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|Episode V]].

Other bounty hunters included [[Skorr/Legends|Skorr]], whom Han dealt with in the [[1981]] [[Star Wars (comic strip)|Star Wars newspaper strip]] distributed by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] and later released as ''[[Classic Star Wars]]''. Han had to deal with bounty hunter [[Alfreda Goot]] in ''[[Scoundrel's Luck]]'', a solitary adventure written by [[Troy Denning]] and published by [[West End Games]] in [[1989]]. There may have been another bounty hunter – an unnamed thug whom Han blasted in a single panel in [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel Comics' ''Star Wars'']], [[Star Wars (1977) 37|issue #37]], from [[1980]].
